published_prior_art.cite: rambachan2023parallel: fixed-M Honest-DiD support/LP sensitivity machinery for aggregate event-study vectors; not the cohort-lift threshold

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov: Markov row-space phase transition, not Honest-DiD aggregation quotient; q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends/p1_baseline_v2: partial DiD pretrend proposal, not aggregate-versus-cohort support sharpness

published_prior_art.cite: liu2025cohortanchored: closest, warns aggregate robust inference can mislead and proposes block-bias workflow, but does not give a primitive quotient equality certificate

repo_prior_art.cite: q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov: singular-value/rank phase transition for event-window support, not cohort-level Honest-DiD breakdown discontinuity

published_prior_art.cite: borusyak2024eventstudy and rothsantanna2023efficient: efficient staggered event-study estimators and aggregation, but no rank-loss discontinuity of sensitivity breakdown frontiers

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The primitive gap is defined as ||J_agg u||_inf - ||J_A u||_inf, but on a one-dimensional active-face reduction support scales as the reciprocal of the seminorm, so Delta_F > 0 implies h_agg < h_coh and M_agg > M_coh, opposite to the stated conservative classification.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the Delta_F sign convention or swap the conservative/anti-conservative labels, then add an explicit one-dimensional active-face lemma showing support equals target scale divided by the active seminorm.
Add a sanity-check corollary for the scalar case q_A(alpha u)=a|alpha| and q_agg(alpha u)=b|alpha|; it should force Gamma=a/b and catch the current sign inversion.
State the Liu and Wright comparisons theorem-by-theorem: identify exactly which estimator or diagnostic each lacks, and contrast it with the proposed primitive quotient certificate.

recommended_next_step: Reject v5 as stated; redraft Conjecture 1 around the reciprocal support/norm sign convention before another novelty review.
